beginning.title: The Beginning
beginning.paragraph1: In 2018, I left my position as a software engineer to pursue building my own vision. The excitement kept me working 18-hour days, sleeping only 4-5 hours. I poured everything into the project for 5-6 months.
beginning.paragraph2: Then I unexpectedly lost access to all my development infrastructure. Everything I'd built was suddenly inaccessible. I had no backups, and even if I had, I couldn't afford to replace the on-premises hardware I'd been using.
beginning.paragraph3: To pay off debts and support my family, I had to return to a salaried job. It took 20 months to regain access to my work. During that time, I drifted far from my dreams, living with uncertainty and mounting financial pressure.

turningPoint.title: The Turning Point
turningPoint.paragraph1: Years later, I kept thinking about what I could do to prevent such a problem from happening again. I needed a tool that would allow me to move my systems to another environment whenever I wanted, activate them whenever needed, back them up easily, and store them securely no matter where I moved them.
turningPoint.paragraph2: In 2021, I discovered Docker through NetworkChuck's video. I was shocked by what was possible. I wished I had known about this technology earlier—it could have changed everything.
turningPoint.paragraph3: But Docker alone didn't solve all my problems. Moving container files between systems was difficult. I faced file ownership compatibility issues, and as data size grew, transfers took longer. Most critically, I had to keep services offline until migration finished, and Docker offered minimal security features.

birth.title: Birth of Rediacc
birth.paragraph1: It all started with developing a portable filesystem logic. From there, I integrated a backup module. Then came one of the most critical challenges: enabling live system transfers with minimal downtime, regardless of data size. I added unique features based on real needs.
birth.paragraph2: This is how Rediacc was born—a product forged through the toughest challenges. Now, it's available to everyone. Use it and don't let anything hold your business back—be ready to survive and accelerate.
birth.quote: When I lost access to my infrastructure, I knew what I had to build: a system that makes disasters impossible.

items.question: Is there a free plan?
items.answer: Yes. The Community plan is free forever with up to 2 machines, 500 monthly repo license issuances, and full snapshot restore capabilities. No credit card required.

items.question: Can I upgrade or downgrade my plan?
items.answer: Yes, you can change your plan at any time. Upgrades take effect immediately, and downgrades will apply at the start of your next billing cycle. No penalties or fees.

items.question: Do you offer annual billing?
items.answer: Yes. Annual billing saves 17% — equivalent to 2 months free. All paid plans are available with monthly or annual billing.

items.question: What payment methods do you accept?
items.answer: We accept all major credit cards (Visa, MasterCard, American Express) and support invoice-based billing for annual Enterprise plans.

items.question: How are machines counted?
items.answer: Each physical or virtual server where Rediacc is installed counts as one machine (floating license). Clones created on those machines do not count separately — only the host machines are counted.

items.question: Can I self-host on any infrastructure?
items.answer: Yes. Rediacc runs on any Linux server with btrfs filesystem support. Bare metal, VMs, and cloud instances (AWS, Azure, GCP, Hetzner, OVH, etc.) are all supported.

items.question: What's included in Community vs Professional?
items.answer: Community includes core cloning, snapshot restores, and community support for up to 2 machines. Professional adds automated scheduling, 24-hour point-in-time restore, priority support, integrations API, and supports up to 5 machines.

items.question: What does Enterprise pricing include?
items.answer: Enterprise plans are customized based on your infrastructure scale, compliance requirements, and support needs. They include dedicated infrastructure, custom SLAs, 24/7/365 support, security reviews, and quarterly readiness exercises.

items.question: Do you offer discounts for education or nonprofits?
items.answer: Yes. Contact us to discuss special pricing for educational institutions and nonprofit organizations.

items.question: Is there a money-back guarantee?
items.answer: Yes. All paid plans include a 14-day money-back guarantee. If Rediacc doesn't meet your needs, we'll issue a full refund — no questions asked.

trustSection.heading: Your Infrastructure. Your Rules.
trustSection.subtitle: Rediacc licenses are designed so you're never locked in. Here's what that means in practice.

rows.title: Your repos run forever.
rows.description: Start, stop, delete, and back up — always works, even years after your subscription ends. These operations never check expiry. Your data is always accessible.

rows.title: Full access to your tools.
rows.description: File sync, VS Code remote, SSH terminal, and all management commands work without any license at all. Completely unrestricted, forever.

rows.title: Only new repos need a subscription.
rows.description: An active subscription is required to create new repositories or fork existing ones. Resize and expand work for 60 days after expiry. Everything else is permanent.

rows.title: 60-day grace for scaling.
rows.description: When your subscription ends, resize and expand continue working for 60 more days. Start, stop, delete, and backup are not affected — they work forever.

rows.title: Unlimited machines. Pay-per-create licensing.
rows.description: A license slot is only held while creating or forking a repository — your daily operations never consume one. Slots auto-release after 1 hour of inactivity, so a 5-slot plan can serve dozens of machines. No per-machine fees, ever.

meta.title: Privacy Policy — Rediacc
meta.description: Learn how Rediacc collects, uses, and protects your personal data. We use cookieless Plausible analytics and store minimal information.
meta.keywords: privacy policy, data protection, GDPR, CCPA, Plausible analytics, Rediacc

introduction.title: 1. Introduction
content: Rediacc ("we", "us", or "our") operates the website www.rediacc.com and the Rediacc infrastructure automation platform. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, disclose, and safeguard your information when you visit our website or use our services.
We are committed to protecting your privacy and handling your data transparently. If you do not agree with the terms of this Privacy Policy, please discontinue use of our website and services.

informationWeCollect.title: 2. Information We Collect
content: We collect minimal information necessary to provide our services. The categories of data we collect depend on how you interact with us.

contactFormData.title: 2.1 Contact Form Data
content: When you submit our contact form, we collect your name, email address, subject of inquiry, and message content. This data is sent directly to our email address and is not stored in a database on our servers.

accountData.title: 2.2 Account Data
content: When you create a Rediacc account to use our platform, we collect your name, email address, organization name, and billing information. Payment processing is handled by our third-party payment processor; we do not store full credit card numbers.

analyticsData.title: 2.3 Analytics Data
content: We use Plausible Analytics, a privacy-focused, cookieless analytics tool. Plausible does not use cookies, does not collect personal data, and is fully compliant with GDPR, CCPA, and PECR. Analytics data includes page views, referral sources, browser type, and country-level location — all without identifying individual visitors.

productTelemetry.title: 2.5 Product Telemetry Data
content: Our software products (Web Application, CLI, Middleware, and Bridge) collect anonymous usage telemetry to improve product quality, performance, and reliability. Telemetry data includes technical information such as application version, operating system, browser type, performance metrics, feature usage patterns, and error reports. Personal identifiers are anonymized before collection — email addresses are reduced to domain only, user IDs are hashed and truncated, and passwords or secrets are never collected.
Telemetry is enabled by default in our Web Application and CLI, and can be disabled in all products. For complete details on what each product collects and how to opt out, see our Telemetry Policy.

cookies.title: 2.4 Cookies
content: We use a single functional cookie (rediacc_lang) to remember your language preference across visits. This cookie is strictly necessary for the website to function as expected and does not track you or collect personal data. For more details, see our Cookie Policy.

howWeUseInformation.title: 3. How We Use Your Information
content: We use the information we collect for the following purposes:
items: To respond to your inquiries submitted through the contact form
To provide, operate, and maintain the Rediacc platform
To process transactions and send related billing information
To send service-related communications (e.g., security updates, maintenance notices)
To understand website usage patterns through aggregated, anonymous analytics
To diagnose errors, improve product performance, and guide product development through anonymized usage telemetry (see our Telemetry Policy)
To comply with legal obligations and enforce our Terms of Service

dataSharing.title: 4. Data Sharing and Third Parties
content: We do not sell, trade, or rent your personal data to third parties. We may share information with:
items: Service providers who assist in operating our platform (e.g., cloud hosting, payment processing), bound by contractual obligations to protect your data
Plausible Analytics for cookieless, anonymized website analytics (no personal data is shared)

Telemetry processing — anonymized product usage data is transmitted to our own telemetry endpoint within the EU for aggregation and analysis. No telemetry data is shared with third parties (see our Telemetry Policy)
Law enforcement if required by law, regulation, or valid legal process

dataRetention.title: 5. Data Retention
content: Contact form submissions are retained in our email system for as long as necessary to respond to your inquiry and maintain correspondence records. Account data is retained for the duration of your active account plus 30 days after account deletion to allow for recovery. Billing records are retained as required by applicable tax and accounting laws.
Plausible analytics data is aggregated and does not contain personal data, so data retention policies do not apply to it.

Product telemetry data is retained as raw traces for up to 90 days for debugging purposes. Aggregated telemetry metrics are retained for up to 12 months and then deleted. For details, see our Telemetry Policy.

yourRights.title: 6. Your Rights
content: Depending on your jurisdiction, you may have the following rights regarding your personal data:
items: Access: Request a copy of the personal data we hold about you
Correction: Request correction of inaccurate or incomplete data
Deletion: Request deletion of your personal data
Portability: Request a machine-readable copy of your data
Objection: Object to the processing of your data for specific purposes
Restriction: Request that we restrict processing of your data
Opt-out of telemetry: Disable product telemetry at any time using the mechanisms described in our Telemetry Policy
content2: To exercise any of these rights, contact us at contact@rediacc.com. We will respond within 30 days.

dataSecurity.title: 7. Data Security
content: We implement industry-standard security measures to protect your data, including encryption in transit (TLS 1.2+), encryption at rest, access controls, and regular security audits. However, no method of electronic storage or transmission is 100% secure, and we cannot guarantee absolute security.

internationalTransfers.title: 8. International Data Transfers
content: Your information may be transferred to and processed in countries other than your country of residence. These countries may have different data protection laws. When we transfer data internationally, we ensure appropriate safeguards are in place, including Standard Contractual Clauses where applicable.

childrenPrivacy.title: 9. Children's Privacy
content: Our services are not directed to individuals under the age of 16. We do not knowingly collect personal data from children. If we become aware that we have collected data from a child under 16, we will delete it promptly.

changes.title: 10. Changes to This Policy

definitions.title: 1. Definitions
content: The following definitions apply throughout these Terms of Service:
items: "Account" means the user account you create to access the Platform and manage your Subscription.
"Authorized User" means any individual granted access to the Services under your Account, including team members, employees, and contractors acting on your behalf.
"Bridge" (Renet) means the Rediacc server-side agent that runs on your Machines to manage encrypted storage, container orchestration, and infrastructure operations.
"CLI" means the Rediacc command-line interface (rdc) installed on your workstation.
"Configuration" means the local configuration files stored on your workstation that contain Machine connection details, encryption credentials, and other operational settings.

"Customer Data" means any data, content, files, containers, configurations, and applications you deploy, store, or process on your Machines using the Services. Customer Data does not include Account information or Telemetry Data.
"Machine" means a server or virtual machine that you own or control on which the Bridge is installed.
"On-Premise Software" means the software components installed on your infrastructure, including the CLI, the Bridge, and the Desktop Application.
"Platform" means the Rediacc web application, account portal, and any cloud-hosted services provided by Rediacc.
"Repository" means an isolated application deployment managed by the Bridge, consisting of an encrypted disk image, a dedicated Docker daemon, and associated containers.
"Services" means, collectively, the On-Premise Software, the Platform, documentation, and any related professional services provided by Rediacc.
"Subscription" means the plan tier you have selected (Community, Professional, Business, or Enterprise), which determines your access to features and resource limits.
"Telemetry Data" means anonymized usage data collected by the Services as described in our Telemetry Policy.

serviceDescription.title: 3. Description of Services
content: Rediacc provides an infrastructure automation platform for managing self-hosted server environments. The Services consist of:
items: On-Premise Software: The CLI (rdc), the Bridge (Renet), and the Desktop Application — installed on your workstations and Machines to orchestrate encrypted, isolated container deployments
Platform: The web application and account portal for subscription management, team collaboration, and (where available) cloud-based infrastructure management
Professional Services: Optional consulting, migration assistance, and implementation support, subject to separate service agreements

accounts.title: 4. Accounts and Registration

telemetry.title: 10. Telemetry and Data Collection
content: The On-Premise Software and Platform collect anonymized Telemetry Data to improve product quality, diagnose errors, and understand usage patterns. dataOwnership.title: 11. Your Data and Encryption

meta.title: Cookie Policy — Rediacc
meta.description: Rediacc's cookie policy. We use one functional cookie for language preference and cookieless Plausible analytics. No tracking cookies.
meta.keywords: cookie policy, cookies, Plausible analytics, GDPR, ePrivacy, Rediacc

introduction.title: 1. Introduction
content: This Cookie Policy explains how Rediacc ("we", "us", or "our") uses cookies on our website www.rediacc.com. We are committed to transparency about the technologies we use and your options for controlling them.

whatAreCookies.title: 2. What Are Cookies?
content: Cookies are small text files stored on your device when you visit a website. They serve various purposes, such as remembering your preferences, enabling functionality, and analyzing website usage. Cookies can be "first-party" (set by the website you visit) or "third-party" (set by external services).

cookiesWeUse.title: 3. Cookies We Use
content: We use a minimal number of cookies. In fact, we use only one:

languageCookie.title: 3.1 Language Preference Cookie
content:
Name: rediacc_lang
Type: First-party, strictly necessary
Duration: 1 year
Purpose: Stores your preferred language (e.g., "en", "de", "fr") so the website displays in your chosen language on return visits.

This cookie is classified as "strictly necessary" under the EU ePrivacy Directive because it is essential for the website to function as you have requested. No consent banner is required for this cookie.

whatWeDontUse.title: 4. What We Don't Use
content: We believe in privacy by default. The following technologies are not used on our website:
items: Tracking cookies — We do not use Google Analytics, Facebook Pixel, or any tracking cookies
Third-party cookies — No external services set cookies on our website
Advertising cookies — We do not serve or track advertisements
Fingerprinting — We do not use browser or device fingerprinting techniques
content2: Note: This Cookie Policy applies to the Rediacc marketing website. Our software products (Web Application, CLI, Middleware, Bridge) collect separate usage telemetry that does not use cookies. For details on product telemetry, see our Telemetry Policy.

analytics.title: 5. Our Analytics Approach
content: We use Plausible Analytics for understanding website usage. Plausible is a privacy-focused analytics tool that:
Because Plausible is cookieless and does not collect personal data, it is compliant with GDPR, CCPA, and PECR without requiring user consent.
items: Does not use cookies of any kind
Does not collect personal data or personally identifiable information
Does not track users across websites or sessions
Is hosted in the EU and is fully GDPR-compliant
Provides only aggregated, anonymous metrics (page views, referral sources, browser types, country-level location)
content2: In addition to website analytics, our software products collect anonymized usage telemetry through OpenTelemetry-based instrumentation. This is separate from website analytics, does not use cookies, and is detailed in our Telemetry Policy.

managingCookies.title: 6. Managing Cookies
content: Since we only use one strictly necessary cookie, there is no cookie consent banner on our website. However, you can manage cookies through your browser settings:
Please note that disabling the rediacc_lang cookie will cause the website to default to English on each visit rather than remembering your language preference.
items: Chrome: Settings → Privacy and security → Cookies and other site data
Firefox: Settings → Privacy & Security → Cookies and Site Data
Safari: Preferences → Privacy → Manage Website Data
Edge: Settings → Cookies and site permissions → Manage and delete cookies

changes.title: 7. Changes to This Policy
content: If we introduce additional cookies in the future, we will update this policy and implement an appropriate consent mechanism as required by law. We will notify you of material changes by posting the updated policy with a new effective date.

contact.title: Questions?
contact.description: If you have questions about our use of cookies, contact us at contact@rediacc.com.

meta.title: Refund Policy — Rediacc
meta.description: Rediacc's refund policy for platform subscriptions and professional services. Covers eligibility, process, and timelines for refund requests.
meta.keywords: refund policy, cancellation, subscription refund, professional services, Rediacc

overview.title: 1. Overview
content: This Refund Policy outlines the terms and conditions for refunds related to Rediacc platform subscriptions and professional services. We want you to be satisfied with our services, and we handle refund requests fairly and transparently.

platformSubscriptions.title: 2. Platform Subscriptions
content: Rediacc offers multiple subscription tiers for our infrastructure automation platform. The following terms apply to paid subscriptions:

freeTier.title: 2.1 Free Tier
content: The Community tier is free forever and does not require payment information. No refund applies as there is no charge.

paidTiers.title: 2.2 Paid Tiers
content: Paid subscriptions (Team, Business, and Enterprise tiers, ranging from $349 to $2,100+/month) are billed in advance on a monthly or annual basis. Because these services are delivered digitally and resources are provisioned immediately upon payment, subscriptions are generally non-refundable.
However, we offer refunds in the following circumstances:
items: First 14 days: If you are a new subscriber and request a refund within 14 days of your first payment, we will issue a full refund. This applies to your first subscription only.
Service outage: If the platform experiences extended downtime (more than 48 consecutive hours) not caused by scheduled maintenance, you may request a pro-rated credit for the affected period.
Billing errors: If you were charged incorrectly (e.g., duplicate charges, wrong tier), we will issue a full refund for the erroneous charge.

annualSubscriptions.title: 2.3 Annual Subscriptions
content: Annual subscriptions may be cancelled at any time. Upon cancellation, you will retain access until the end of your current billing period. No partial refunds are issued for the remaining months of an annual subscription, except within the 14-day window for first-time subscribers.

professionalServices.title: 3. Professional Services
content: Rediacc offers professional services including architecture reviews, migration assistance, custom integration development, and dedicated support, billed at hourly rates ($400–$1,200/hr depending on the service package). The following refund terms apply:
items: Before work begins: Full refund if you cancel before any work has been performed
During engagement: You will be billed only for hours worked. Any unused prepaid hours will be refunded

Quality disputes: If you are dissatisfied with the quality of work, contact us within 7 days of delivery. We will work to resolve the issue first; if resolution is not possible, we may offer a partial refund at our discretion
Minimum engagement: Some service packages have a minimum commitment. Cancellation before the minimum period may incur a cancellation fee as specified in your service agreement

howToRequest.title: 4. How to Request a Refund
content: To request a refund, email contact@rediacc.com with the following information:
items: Your account email address
The subscription or service for which you are requesting a refund
The reason for your refund request
Any relevant order or invoice numbers
content2: We aim to respond to all refund requests within 5 business days.

refundProcessing.title: 5. Refund Processing
content: Approved refunds will be processed to the original payment method within 10 business days. The actual time for the refund to appear on your statement depends on your payment provider and may take an additional 5–10 business days.
Refunds for annual subscriptions paid by wire transfer may take up to 30 business days to process.

nonRefundable.title: 6. Non-Refundable Items
content: The following are not eligible for refunds:
items: Subscriptions cancelled after the 14-day new subscriber window
Usage-based charges (e.g., overage fees) for resources already consumed
Professional services hours that have already been delivered and accepted
Account reactivation fees
Charges resulting from your violation of the Terms of Service

changes.title: 7. Changes to This Policy
content: We may update this Refund Policy from time to time. Changes will be posted on this page with an updated effective date. The policy in effect at the time of your purchase applies to that transaction.

contact.title: Questions?
contact.description: If you have questions about refunds or need assistance, contact us at contact@rediacc.com.

meta.title: Telemetry Policy — Rediacc
meta.description: Details on what telemetry data Rediacc products collect, why, how it is processed, and how to opt out. Covers Web App, CLI, Middleware, and Bridge.
meta.keywords: telemetry policy, data collection, OpenTelemetry, opt-out, privacy, GDPR, Rediacc

introduction.title: 1. Introduction
content: Rediacc software products collect anonymized usage telemetry to help us improve product quality, diagnose issues, and understand how our tools are used. This Telemetry Policy describes exactly what data is collected, why, how it is processed, and how you can opt out.

This policy covers four products: the Rediacc Web Application, the Rediacc CLI, the Rediacc Middleware, and the Rediacc Bridge (Renet). Website analytics (Plausible) are covered separately in our Privacy Policy and Cookie Policy.

principles.title: 2. Our Telemetry Principles
content: All telemetry collection at Rediacc follows these principles:
items: Privacy by design: Personal identifiers are anonymized before transmission. Email addresses are reduced to domain only, user IDs are hashed (SHA-256) and truncated, passwords and secrets are never collected.
Minimal collection: We collect only what is necessary to improve product quality and diagnose issues. We do not collect file contents, database contents, query results, or business data.
Transparency: This policy lists every category of data collected by each product. There are no hidden data flows.
User control: Every product provides a mechanism to disable telemetry entirely. Opt-out is respected immediately and completely.
Aggregation: Telemetry data is analyzed in aggregate. We do not build individual user profiles from telemetry.

legalBasis.title: 3. Legal Basis for Collection
content: Under the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and the ePrivacy Directive, our legal basis for telemetry collection depends on the product context:

For the Web Application (browser-based): telemetry is collected under legitimate interest (GDPR Art. 6(1)(f)) for the purpose of maintaining and improving service quality. You can opt out at any time.

For the CLI, Middleware, and Bridge (installed on your infrastructure): telemetry is collected under legitimate interest with clear opt-out mechanisms provided. You can disable telemetry before any data is sent.
For users in jurisdictions requiring explicit consent (e.g., South Korea under PIPA), telemetry should be opted into explicitly through the product configuration before any data is transmitted.

webApp.title: 4. Web Application Telemetry
content: The Rediacc Web Application uses OpenTelemetry-based distributed tracing. Telemetry is transmitted to rediacc.com. In production, only 10% of sessions are sampled.

webDataCollected.title: 4.1 Data Collected
content: The Web Application collects the following categories of data:
items: Browser information: browser name and version, preferred language, platform (e.g., Windows, macOS, Linux), screen resolution, color depth, user agent string, device memory (if available), network connection type
User context: session ID (randomly generated per session), email domain only (local part is redacted, e.g., ***@example.com), organization name, team name
Page views and navigation: pages visited within the application, route changes, referrer information
User interactions: clicks, form submissions, modal opens/closes, search queries, filter changes, tab switches, file downloads (file names are not collected)
API calls: HTTP method, URL path, response status code, request duration
Errors: error type, error message, stack trace (truncated to 1,000 characters), error source
Performance metrics: Core Web Vitals (LCP, FID, CLS, TTFB, INP), resource timing, memory usage (sampled every 30 seconds), long task detection
Component lifecycle: component mount/unmount timing, render duration

webNotCollected.title: 4.2 Data NOT Collected
content: The Web Application does not collect:
items: Full email addresses (local part is always redacted)
Passwords, tokens, or authentication credentials
File contents, database contents, or query results
Form field values (only the fact that a form was submitted is recorded)
Clipboard contents or keystrokes
IP addresses (not logged by our telemetry endpoint)

webOptOut.title: 4.3 How to Opt Out
content: Set the userConsent configuration flag to false in your account settings. Telemetry stops immediately upon opt-out.
Desktop Application: Telemetry is completely disabled in the Desktop (Electron) application by default. No data is collected or transmitted.

cli.title: 5. CLI Telemetry
content: The Rediacc CLI uses the OpenTelemetry Node SDK. Telemetry is transmitted to rediacc.com.

cliDataCollected.title: 5.1 Data Collected
content: The CLI collects the following categories of data:
items: Command usage: command name, CLI version, exit code, success or failure status
Anonymized arguments: command options with sensitive values redacted — passwords, tokens, secrets, API keys, and credentials are replaced with [REDACTED]
Environment: operating system type, Node.js runtime version, deployment environment
Error information: error type, error message, and limited stack trace when commands fail
API interactions: HTTP method, URL path, response status code, request duration
Session: randomly generated session ID, command execution duration
Performance profiling: CPU usage samples and memory allocation profiles, collected automatically and sent to our profiling endpoint

cliOptOut.title: 5.2 How to Opt Out
content: You can disable CLI telemetry (including profiling) using any of these methods:
items: Set the environment variable REDIACC_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
Set telemetryEnabled: false in your CLI configuration file
Telemetry is automatically disabled in CI environments (detected via the CI environment variable)

middleware.title: 6. Middleware Telemetry
content: The Rediacc Middleware collects operational telemetry. Telemetry is transmitted to rediacc.com.

middlewareDataCollected.title: 6.1 Data Collected
content: The Middleware collects the following categories of data:
items: System information: hostname, operating system version, .NET runtime version
API calls: HTTP method, endpoint path, response status code, request duration
Database operations: stored procedure name, execution duration, row count returned (no query content, parameters, or result data)
Authentication events: hashed user identifier only — user IDs are hashed with SHA-256 and a salt, then truncated to the first 12 characters. Original user IDs are never transmitted.
Health checks: service component availability status and response times
Application lifecycle: startup, shutdown, and configuration change events
Error information: error type, error message, and operational context

middlewareOptOut.title: 6.2 How to Opt Out
content: Set Telemetry:Enabled to false in your Middleware configuration (appsettings.json or environment-specific configuration).

bridge.title: 7. Bridge (Renet) Telemetry
content: The Rediacc Bridge (Renet) uses OpenTelemetry with gzip-compressed OTLP export. Telemetry is transmitted to rediacc.com in batches (maximum 10 spans, flushed every 5 seconds).

bridgeDataCollected.title: 7.1 Data Collected
content: The Bridge collects the following categories of data:
items: Queue operations: task ID, priority level, operation type, execution duration, success or failure status
SSH operations: target host, username, port number, exit code, execution duration (no command content or output is collected)
API calls: procedure name, request duration
Vault operations: operation type and duration (no secret values, keys, or vault contents are collected)
Bridge lifecycle: startup, shutdown, and reconnection events
Performance profiling: CPU usage samples and memory allocation profiles, collected automatically and sent to our profiling endpoint

bridgeOptOut.title: 7.2 How to Opt Out
content: Set the environment variable REDIACC_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1 to disable Bridge telemetry (including profiling).

dataProcessing.title: 8. Data Processing and Storage
content: All telemetry data is transmitted over encrypted connections (TLS 1.2+) to our telemetry endpoint. Data is processed and stored as follows:
items: Encryption: All data is encrypted in transit using TLS. The Bridge additionally uses gzip compression.
Retention: Raw telemetry traces are stored for up to 90 days for debugging and incident investigation. Aggregated metrics are retained for up to 12 months and then deleted.
Access: Telemetry data is accessible only to Rediacc engineering and operations staff on a need-to-know basis.
No third-party sharing: Telemetry data is not shared with, sold to, or accessible by any third party.
Infrastructure: All telemetry data is processed and stored on infrastructure located within the European Union.

internationalTransfers.title: 9. International Data Transfers
content: Telemetry data is processed and stored exclusively on servers within the European Union. In the unlikely event that telemetry data needs to be transferred outside the EU/EEA (e.g., for disaster recovery), appropriate safeguards will be in place, including Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) as approved by the European Commission.

yourRights.title: 10. Your Rights
content: Because our telemetry data is anonymized and aggregated, it generally does not constitute personal data under GDPR. However, to the extent that any telemetry data can be linked to you personally, you have the right to:
items: Access: Request information about what telemetry data we hold that may relate to you
Deletion: Request deletion of any telemetry data that can be linked to you
Opt-out: Disable telemetry at any time using the product-specific mechanisms described above
Complaint: Lodge a complaint with the Estonian Data Protection Inspectorate (Andmekaitse Inspektsioon) or your local supervisory authority
content2: To exercise any of these rights, contact us at contact@rediacc.com. We will respond within 30 days.

ccpa.title: 11. California Residents (CCPA/CPRA)
content: If you are a California resident, the following additional disclosures apply under the California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) as amended by the California Privacy Rights Act (CPRA):
items: Categories of information collected: Internet or electronic network activity information (browser type, interaction data, error logs), device identifiers (anonymized), geolocation data (country-level only, derived from IP address which is not stored)
Purpose: Improving and maintaining service quality, debugging, performance monitoring, and security
Sale/Sharing: We do not sell or share telemetry data as defined by the CCPA/CPRA
Right to opt out: Use the product-specific opt-out mechanisms described in sections 4–7 above. We also honor the Global Privacy Control (GPC) signal in our Web Application.

changes.title: 12. Changes to This Policy
content: We may update this Telemetry Policy when we add new products, change what data is collected, or modify our processing practices. Changes will be posted on this page with an updated effective date. We will notify users of material changes through our product release notes and website.

contact.title: Questions?
contact.description: If you have questions about our telemetry practices or need help opting out, contact us at contact@rediacc.com.
